alias.description: Define or display aliases. -g: global alias (expanded anywhere). -r: regular alias. -s: suffix alias (file extension handler).

autoload.description: Mark function for autoloading from fpath. -U: suppress alias expansion. -z: zsh-style loading. Functions loaded on first call.

bindkey.description: Bind keys to ZLE widgets. -e: emacs keymap. -v: vi keymap. -M: specify keymap. -l: list keymaps.

builtin.description: Execute a builtin command, ignoring functions and aliases with the same name.

cd.description: Change directory. With two args, replaces 'old' with 'new' in $PWD. cd - goes to $OLDPWD.

compdef.description: Associate a completion function with commands. Part of the completion system.

declare.description: Same as typeset. Declare variables with attributes.

dirs.description: Display directory stack. -v: verbose listing with indices. -c: clear stack.

echo.description: Print arguments. -n: no trailing newline. -e: interpret escapes. Use print for more control.

emulate.description: Set shell emulation mode: zsh, sh, ksh, csh. -L: local to function. -R: reset all options.

eval.description: Concatenate args and execute as a command. Arguments undergo expansion twice.

exec.description: Replace shell with command. With no command, redirections affect current shell.

export.description: Mark variables for export to child processes. Same as typeset -gx.

fc.description: Fix command — list, edit, or re-execute history entries.

functions.description: Display or set attributes on functions. Same as typeset -f.

hash.description: Manage command hash table. Maps command names to paths for faster lookup.

integer.description: Declare integer variables. Arithmetic is performed on assignments.

kill.description: Send signal to process or job. -l: list signals. -9: SIGKILL.

let.description: Evaluate arithmetic expression. Same as (( expr )).

local.description: Declare local variables in functions. Same as typeset in a function context.

popd.description: Remove directory from stack and cd to new top.

print.description: Print arguments. More powerful than echo. -P: prompt expansion. -f: printf format. -l: one per line.

pushd.description: Push directory onto stack and cd to it.

read.description: Read a line of input. -r: raw (no backslash). -s: silent. -t: timeout. -A: into array. -k n: read n chars.

readonly.description: Mark variables as read-only. Cannot be changed or unset.

rehash.description: Rebuild command hash table. Run after installing new programs.

return.description: Return from a function with exit status n (default: status of last command).

set.description: Set shell options and positional parameters. -e: exit on error. -u: error on unset.

setopt.description: Enable shell options. Options control shell behavior: globbing, history, cd, correction, etc.

shift.description: Shift positional parameters left by n (default: 1).

source.description: Read and execute commands from file in current shell. Same as '.' command.

test.description: Evaluate conditional expression. Prefer [[ ]] for advanced features.

trap.description: Execute action when shell receives signal. Common: EXIT, INT, TERM, ERR, DEBUG.

type.description: Show how a name would be interpreted: alias, function, builtin, command.

typeset.description: Declare and set attributes on variables. The most versatile variable declaration command.

umask.description: Get or set file creation mask.

wait.description: Wait for background jobs/processes to complete.

whence.description: Show how each name would be interpreted. -p: search path only. -v: verbose.

where.description: Display all locations of a command.

zcompile.description: Compile zsh scripts to wordcode for faster loading.

zle.description: Zsh Line Editor — manage widgets. -N: create widget from function. -C: create completion widget.

zmodload.description: Load or unload zsh modules. Common: zsh/datetime, zsh/stat, zsh/regex, zsh/net/tcp.

zparseopts.description: Parse command-line options. -D: remove parsed options. -E: don't stop at first non-option. -A assoc: store in associative array.

zstyle.description: Define styles for the completion system and other subsystems. Pattern: ':completion:function:completer:command:argument:tag'.

PROMPT.description: Primary prompt string. Supports: %n (user), %m (host), %~ (dir), %F{color}...%f (color), %B...%b (bold), %# (# for root, %% for user).

RPROMPT.description: Right-side prompt string. Commonly shows time, git status, or exit code.

PS2.description: Continuation prompt (shown for multi-line commands). Default: '%_> '.

HISTFILE.description: Path to the history file. Default: ~/.zsh_history.

HISTSIZE.description: Maximum number of history entries in memory.

SAVEHIST.description: Number of history entries saved to HISTFILE.

FPATH.description: Function search path (colon-separated). Directories where autoloaded functions are found.

fpath.description: Array form of FPATH. Tied to FPATH — changes to one affect the other.

path.description: Array form of PATH. Tied to PATH.

CDPATH.description: Directories searched by cd. cd target tries CDPATH entries as parents.

KEYTIMEOUT.description: Timeout for key sequences (1/100th second). Default: 40 (0.4s). Set to 1 for fast vi-mode switch.

WORDCHARS.description: Characters considered part of a word for word-based operations. Default includes many special chars.
